description: A heap buffer overflow vulnerability exists in V8's write barrier mechanism that allows an attacker to bypass the V8 sandbox. The root cause is a missing bounds check in `BasicSlotSet::Insert()` (`src/heap/base/basic-slot-set.h:117`): the function computes `bucket_index = slot_offset / (kBitsPerBucket * SlotGranularity)` from the caller-supplied `slot_offset` and directly accesses `buckets()[bucket_index]` without verifying that `bucket_index` is less than the number of buckets allocated for the page's SlotSet. The triggering condition is as follows: (1) The attacker uses `Sandbox.MemoryView` to corrupt the `length` field of a `JSArray` object (an in-sandbox field, e.g., setting it to 0x8000 = 32768 while the actual elements capacity is only 5). (2) A subsequent keyed store to the array (e.g., via `KeyedStoreIC_Megamorphic` / `SetKeyedPropertyHandler`) triggers the generational write barrier (`RecordWriteSaveFP`). (3) The write barrier builtin (`InsertIntoRememberedSet`) computes `slot_offset = slot - MemoryChunkFromAddress(object)`, where `slot` falls outside the 256KB page of `object` due to the corrupted length causing element accesses or backing-store allocations beyond page boundaries. (4) `InsertIntoRememberedSetFromCode` is called with this oversized `slot_offset` (e.g., 0x4D000), which maps to `bucket_index = 77` while the SlotSet only has 64 buckets (512 bytes for a standard 256KB page). (5) `BasicSlotSet::Insert(slot_offset)` reads 8 bytes at 104 bytes past the end of the 512-byte SlotSet allocation — memory outside the V8 sandbox — constituting a sandbox violation.

fixes.evidence_text: 47c50e8013d9061ca113e66514568debc4dfb133 [heap, builtins] Check bucket bounds for remembered set
fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/6011212 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
